scenario.scale: IT service provider for home office, municipal workplaces and medical practice environments, 95 employees
scenario.context: Ahead of the next external audit, a scheduled permission review is due for the service portal, document repository and support mailbox because role changes, substitutions and legacy accounts were only partially updated.
scenario.mission: Define review scope, critical accounts, exceptions, permission revocation and evidence for the current access status.

source.note: Based on recent role changes in support, temporary substitutions, still active legacy accounts and the requirement to confirm approved access per system in a traceable way.

station.profile: Access review, role changes, exceptions and evidence
constraints: Person binding, role need and system access must be confirmed, adjusted or revoked per account
Substitutions and exceptions may only remain with a reason, end date or follow-up review
For audit and team leadership a readable closure with decisions, implementation and residual points is required

customerRequest.subject: Prepare access review for service portal and DMS by Friday
customerRequest.body:
Good day,

before the external audit we must cleanly straighten the access status for the service portal, document repository and support mailbox. In recent weeks there were role changes in support, several substitutions without a clear end date, and still active accounts from an expired partner project.

Please prepare a reliable status by Friday: which accounts must be clarified immediately, which permissions can be revoked or reduced, which exceptions are justified and which evidence we will provide.

Kind regards
Domain department

contentBlocks.text: SecuBridge reviews the current access status for several operational areas before the audit. The critical point is not the number of accounts, but whether current role, domain need and technical permission still match for each person.

rows: Role changes in support, old profiles still active in parallel
Former project accounts and expired partner access still visible
Read access after substitutions not fully cleaned up
Elevated rights from earlier maintenance are under review
A clear decision per account or access with evidence and residual point

contentBlocks.text: The goal is not a large list export, but a review that consolidates standard cases, special rights, exceptions and technical implementation into a traceable working status.

questions.title: Review priorities
questions.prompt: Which accounts or situations must be clarified first in the access review? Several points can be critical at the same time.

options.text: Accounts of departed staff or expired project partners that are still active.
options.rationale: Legacy accounts without current need are among the first review cases.

options.text: Substitution or emergency rights without end date or clear approval.
options.rationale: Temporary additional rights quickly lose legitimacy without limits.

options.text: Role changes where old and new permissions remained in parallel.
options.rationale: Parallel old and new roles often expand access unnoticed.

options.text: Shared or no longer clearly attributable accounts in the support context.
options.rationale: Without clear person binding there is no reliable accountability later.

options.text: Accounts in unchanged standard profiles should be reviewed in depth first; special rights and legacy accounts can follow later.
options.rationale: Priority here is on abnormal or overly broad permissions, not quiet standard cases.

options.text: A shared mailbox can be excluded from the review as long as at least one responsible person has access.
options.rationale: Shared access also needs a clear and current permission logic.

feedback.correct: Critical cases for the review start were prioritized correctly.
feedback.wrong: Focus on legacy accounts, special rights, role changes and missing person binding.

questions.title: Review approach for Friday
questions.prompt: What most clearly indicates a reliable access review here?

options.text: Export all rights once and store the list as evidence; domain confirmations can follow later.
options.rationale: An export alone shows neither current need nor the decision per account.

options.text: For each system, check accounts, current role, actual need, special rights and exceptions against each other and document the decision per access.
options.rationale: This is how technical lists, domain need and reliable decisions are connected.

options.text: Only review admin and partner accounts, because standard roles rarely matter for the audit.
options.rationale: Standard roles also belong to the review when role and need must be verified.

options.text: Deactivate all accounts as a precaution and then clarify in daily business who gets access again.
options.rationale: That causes uncontrolled disruption instead of a governed, traceable review.

feedback.correct: The reliable review approach was correctly identified.
feedback.wrong: The target is a process that ties system list, role need, special rights and documented decisions together.

questions.title: Align systems, roles and evidence cleanly
questions.subject: Build review matrix for critical areas
questions.body:
Hello everyone,

before I hand the status to team leadership, I need a clear statement per area on what exactly is reviewed and which first evidence will carry the decision later. Please do not split service portal, document repository and elevated access apart, but combine them into one shared review picture.

Kind regards
Access governance

questions.title: Focus and evidence per area
questions.prompt: For each area, add the most important review focus and the first appropriate evidence.

rows.label: Service portal / focus
rows.expected: Check current team role, required access scope and remaining old rights after role changes.
options: Check current team role, required access scope and remaining old rights after role changes.
Only check whether the password was changed recently.
Sort the latest citizen requests by urgency.
Only count how long the account already exists.

rows.label: Service portal / evidence
rows.expected: Role export with account owner, current profile and domain confirmation from team leadership.
options: Role export with account owner, current profile and domain confirmation from team leadership.
Screenshot of a chat thread about spontaneous substitutions.
Calendar entry for a team meeting without account reference.
General org chart slide without access status.

rows.label: Document repository / focus
rows.expected: Check former project accounts, expired partner access and temporary substitutions against the current project status.
options: Check former project accounts, expired partner access and temporary substitutions against the current project status.
Only compare the used storage size of the folders.
Review only the file names from the last week.
Standardize the folder color of the most important projects.

rows.label: Document repository / evidence
rows.expected: Folder permission list matched to project staffing with marked legacy accounts.
options: Folder permission list matched to project staffing with marked legacy accounts.
Only a file overview sorted by date.
A photo of the whiteboard from the last project meeting.
A general list of all open tasks without folder reference.

rows.label: Gateway / focus
rows.expected: Elevated rights must exist only with person binding, approval and time limitation.
options: Elevated rights must exist only with person binding, approval and time limitation.
It is enough if the accounts are still technically functional.
Most important is whether the last maintenance seemed successful.
The number of all administrators should remain constant over several months.

rows.label: Gateway / evidence
rows.expected: Approval document or ticket with account name, purpose, time limit and technical implementation.
options: Approval document or ticket with account name, purpose, time limit and technical implementation.
Short note that the partner has worked with the system for a long time.
Screenshot of the gateway start page.
General maintenance planning without reference to specific accounts.

feedback.correct: Review focus and first evidence were matched consistently per area.
feedback.wrong: Check which focus actually clarifies permissions and which evidence is more than context or habit.

questions.title: Responsibilities in the review
questions.prompt: Which role takes which initial responsibility in the access review?

pairs.right: Confirm current domain need and required standard roles per person
Prepare account and role exports and implement revocation or downgrades technically
Assess special rights, partner access and shared accounts with high risk
Consolidate decisions, exceptions, residual points and evidence storage in the closure

options: Confirm current domain need and required standard roles per person
Prepare account and role exports and implement revocation or downgrades technically
Assess special rights, partner access and shared accounts with high risk
Consolidate decisions, exceptions, residual points and evidence storage in the closure

feedback.correct: The roles were matched with their initial responsibilities.
feedback.wrong: Focus on who confirms need, who implements technically, who assesses risk and who consolidates the closure.

questions.title: Immediate actions in numbers
questions.prompt: In the service portal, 41 active accounts are reviewed. 28 remain unchanged, 4 remain as temporary substitutions with an end date and 3 still require domain clarification. How many accounts can be revoked or downgraded directly in the review?

feedback.correct: The directly actionable amount was calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please subtract confirmed, temporarily continued and still open cases from all active accounts.

questions.title: Prepare closure for audit and team leadership
questions.subject: Complete access review reliably by Friday
questions.body:
Hello everyone,

for the audit I do not need a large rights export, but a readable closure on which accounts are confirmed, revoked, downgraded or temporarily continued as exceptions. Please also make visible which open points remain and when these will be reviewed again.

Kind regards
Audit

questions.title: Closure documents for audit
questions.prompt: Which documents or results must be available after the access review so audit and team leadership can understand the status?

options.text: A review list per account or access with decision and short justification.
options.rationale: Without documented decisions, the status is not traceable later.

options.text: Evidence of which revocations or downgrades were already implemented technically.
options.rationale: Closure must show implementation, not only intent.

options.text: An exceptions list with reason, responsible person and end date or follow-up review.
options.rationale: Temporary deviations must not silently slip into normal operations.

options.text: Open residual points with responsible persons and the next review date.
options.rationale: Open cases must remain visible for audit and leadership.

options.text: A full screenshot of all user lists without any evaluation.
options.rationale: Without decision, implementation and justification the screenshot lacks meaning.

options.text: A short email stating that access now looks clean overall.
options.rationale: An impression does not replace a reliable review closure.

feedback.correct: The required closure documents were fully identified.
feedback.wrong: Focus on decisions, technical implementation, exceptions and open residual points rather than raw lists or impressions.

questions.title: Clean review sequence
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order so the access review can be closed reliably.
items: Collect affected systems, account types and reference lists for the review.
Review flagged accounts, role changes, partner access and exceptions from a domain perspective.
Decide per account on revocation, downgrade, confirmation or temporary continuation.
Verify technical implementation and evidence storage.
Release the closure with residual points and follow-up review for audit and team leadership.
correctOrder: Collect affected systems, account types and reference lists for the review.
Review flagged accounts, role changes, partner access and exceptions from a domain perspective.
Decide per account on revocation, downgrade, confirmation or temporary continuation.
Verify technical implementation and evidence storage.
Release the closure with residual points and follow-up review for audit and team leadership.

feedback.correct: The order leads cleanly from scope to audit-ready closure.
feedback.wrong: First scope and review cases, then decision per account, then implementation and only then the closure.
